metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4980
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1a-d: double immunofluorescence labeling of DJ-1 (PARK7) and synaptophysin (SYP) or synaptobrevin2 (VAMP2) in mouse cortical neurons showed extensive co-localization.

Figure 1e: immunogold labeling for DJ-1 by electron microscopy (EM) was carried out in WT and DJ-1 KO mouse brains. Particles in the presynapse seem to co-localize mostly with synaptic vesicles in WT, no particles were observed in knockout controls.

Figure 1f, S2: "Superresolution imaging also revealed that GFP-DJ-1 localization partially overlapped with or was adjacent to (i.e., within several hundred nanometers) that of VAMP2-mCherry (Fig. 1F and Fig. S2), suggesting that DJ-1 localizes to nerve terminals and may participate in synaptic functions, such as SV trafficking.". Experimental description: From results, Figure 1 legend: "Representative images of DJ-1 and synaptophysin immunocytochemistry. Mouse cortical neurons were fixed and double-stained with anti-DJ-1 (green) and antisynaptophysin (red) antibodies"

M&M:
"pEGFP - DJ-1 was generated using PCR. All constructs were verified by DNA sequencing. pSyn-ATP is gifted from Timothy A. Ryan, Weill Cornell Medical College, New York, NY (3). The anti - DJ-1 antibody was purchased from BD Bioscience and antisynaptophysin antibody from Synaptic System."
